LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT BAY AREA DESPERADOS MOTORCYCLE CLUB'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION PLEADED AGAINST IT IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. UNDISPUTED FACTS POSED BY PLAINTIFF APPEAR TO ESTABLISH THAT DEFENDANT WAS CONSCIOUS OF DUTY AND THE OF ESCORTING OF PEOPLE PAST THE SEARCH POINT, WHICH WAS A SECURITY PROTOCOL TO KEEP PROHIBITED ITEMS OUT OF THE CLUB. THERE IS ALSO EVIDENCE THAT A CLUB REPRESENTATIVE ESCORTED PEOPLE THROUGH SECURITY. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489 (302/EHG)

Defendant Bay Area Desperados Motorcycle Club'S Motion For Summary Judgment As To All Causes Of Action Pleaded Against It In The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adante D. Pointer, of Law Offices of John L. Burris, Tel. 510.839.5200, for plaintiffs;
J. Randall Andrada, of Andrada & Associates, P.C., Tel. 510.287.4160, for defendants;
Terrence J. Brasch, of Andrada & Associates, P.C., Tel. 510.287.4160, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court required parties to appear. Defendant Bay Area Desperados Motorcycle Club's Motion For Summary Judgment As To All Causes Of Action Pleaded Against It In The First Amended Complaint is denied. Undisputed facts posed by plaintiff appear to establish that defendant was conscious of duty and the of escorting of people past the search point, which was a security protocol to keep prohibited items out of the club. There is also evidence that a club representative escorted people through security. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
